section.title: GMAT R2026a Release Notes
section.para: The General Mission Analysis Tool (GMAT) version R2026a was released in April 2026 and is the 16th release for the project. The previous public release was in April 2025. This is a major project release that includes numerous improvements and enhancements in general capabilities and models, mission design and orbit determination functionality, visualization, and improved cross-platform compatibility.
Below, we give a summary of key changes in this release.

section.title: Milestones and Accomplishments
section.para: We are excited that GMAT continues to see significant adoption for operational mission support.

listitem.para: GMAT is being used as the primary operational tool for maneuver planning by the Space weather Observations at L1 to Advance Readiness – 1 (SOLAR-1) flight dynamics team.
GMAT is being used as the primary operational tool for maneuver planning by the Roman Space Telescope (RST) flight dynamics team.
GMAT orbit determination is the primary tool used by the NASA GSFC Flight Dynamics Facility (FDF) to support various missions in LEO, GEO, Lunar, Lagrange point, and other orbit regimes.

section.para: For the first time in GMAT release history, the https://github.com/nasa/GMAT GitHub repo will soon be used to disseminate select code updates to prior to the next public release.

section.title: General Capabilities and Models
section.para: The following general capabilities are new to R2026a:

listitem.para: Enable exponential atmospheres at all solar system bodies (GMT-8449, GMT-8160). GMAT’s drag modeling can now model atmospheres at any solar system body, using an exponential model based on the exponential drag model found in Fundamentals of Astrodynamics and Applications by David Vallado. Users need to supply a file containing the coefficients for the model. Examples of this file for the Earth and Mars can be found in the data/atmosphere folders in the release. See the Ex_ForceModels.script in the application samples directory for an example of this capability.

Upgrade the ground track component to be extendable and work on macOS (GMT-8451, GMT-8507). The GMAT GroundTrackPlot component has been replaced by a custom GroundTrack component. The new component is scripted identically to the older component and is identified by the object name GroundTrack in script. Scripts that use GroundTrackPlot as the object type being created now create the replacement component rather than the older type. GroundTrack objects fill their windows completely, rather than preserving a 2:1 aspect ratio.

Add the MSISE-00 Atmospheric Model to the public release (GMT-8487). GMAT now includes the 2001 United States Naval Research Laboratory Mass Spectrometer and Incoherent Scatter Radar Exosphere (NRLMSISE00) atmospheric model for use in drag modeling. See the Ex_ForceModels script in the application samples directory for an example of this capability.

Replace OrbitView with OpenFramesInterface (OFI) by mapping the OV scripting to an OFI window (GMT-8322). GMAT, using a new default libOVtoOFI plugin, automatically maps OrbitView 3d visualization objects to an OpenFramesInterface window. OrbitView is deprecated and subject to removal in a future release, but users can for now disable the libOVtoOFI plugin in the startup file if they want to use the deprecated OrbitView.

Update SPICE inputs readers and writers to treat MJ2000Eq and ICRF coordinate systems correctly (GMT-5211, GMT-8468, GMT-8300). GMAT will now properly interpret SPICE planetary ephemeris files and other SPICE ephemeris files as ICRF. GMAT will also transform states to ICRF when writing SPICE files. As a result of this, users may notice slight differences in the results of computations, including propagation using multiple point-masses, orbit determination, and coordinate transformations involving translations to other bodies.

Fix Lagrange Interpolator for intermediate points and ascending/descending data (GMT-8443). Updated the embedded Lagrange interpolator to properly interpolate data where the independent variable (e.g., time for an ephem or mass for a center of mass file) is either ascending or descending.

Error and exit when a user specifies a non-existing startup file (GMT-7802). GMAT issues an error and exits when using –startup_file command line argument if the specified startup file does not exist.

Enable third bodies for SRP shadow calculations (GMT-6543, GMT-8403). A new ForceModel parameter, SRP.ExtraShadowBodies is now available. This can be used to model SRP shadow effects due to bodies in addition to the force model central body, which is always considered for shadow modeling. See the Ex_ForceModel_ExtraShadowBodies script in the application samples directory.
Refactor the ShadowState code (GMT-8077). The GMAT code associated with performing percent shadow calculations was refactored to be more maintainable.

Increase the parameters a user can "Get" in the MissionSequence (GMT-8111, GMT-8363). Even though GMAT has the ability for users to configure resource object (e.g. Spacecraft, Propagator, DC, Optimizers, Impulsive or FiniteBurns) parameters before the mission sequence starts, a user cannot get all of those object parameters in the mission sequence. This work expands the parameters a user can retrieve during the mission sequence run. GMT-8111 and GMT-8363 details the impacted parameters.

Generalizing the use of the Intrusion Locator (GMT-7572, GMT-8332, GMT-8482). The intrusion locator introduced in GMAT R2022a has been enhanced to allow sensor intrusion calculations for bodies that are not the central body of the model. This capability enables the calculation of transits and occultations as seen by a spacecraft. See the Ex_IntrusionLocator_Mercury_Sun_Transit script in the application samples directory.

Fix ground station mask file issue with conflicting NAIF Ids (GMT-8416). Internal NAIF ID generation for FOV frames used by ground stations in a ContactLocator have been updated to ensure unique values when numerous ground stations are used. This prevents one FOV being overwritten by another within SPICE from matching IDs.

Add logic for ephemeris generation when steps in the segment are less than the interpolation order (GMT-8238). The ephemeris propagation code in GMAT now reduces that the order of the state interpolator to match the number of points available in each ephemeris segment, so that interpolated states can be generated for segments that do not have enough points for the scripted interpolation. When the interpolation uses fewer points than needed for the scripted interpolation, GMAT posts a message in the message window identifying the segment and the interpolation order used in that segment.

Update CSALT interface scripts and add tutorials (GMT-8298). Additional guidance have been added to show users how to create a CSALT project in Visual Studio, describe the main CSALT C++ base classes that must be inherited to use the tool, and six example tutorials. Each tutorial sequentially adds additional CSALT features available to create and solve optimal control problems.

Add built-In quaternion functions (GMT-8110, GMT-8371). Three new quaternion functions are now available in GMAT: QuaternionRotation, QuaternionProduct, and QuaternionToDCM. The use of these functions can be found in the Users Guide.

Enhance ability to use SPICE for coordinate system transformations (GMT-8255, GMT-8300). Reference frames defined in the SPICE toolkit may now be used in GMAT by creating a coordinate system using the SPICE Axes type. Useful examples of this are Moon Principal Axes (PA) and Mean Earth (ME) frames, but the IAU frames for other bodies can also be defined and employed through assignment of the appropriate kernels. See the Ex_SPICECoordinateAxes script in the application samples directory.

Update help system by replacing the .chm file with .html (Tutorial/User Guide) (GMT-8500). Previous versions of GMAT utilized Compiled HTML Help (CHM) files but this file format is obsolete and not supported, so GMAT releases moving forward will point to the HTML files provided.

section.title: Beta- and Alpha-level Capabilities
section.para: These are new features that have not yet undergone full testing and may have bugs. To access some of these features, you may need to edit the bin/gmat_startup_file.txt file to load the desired alpha plug-in and/or activate features only available in testing mode (set RUN_MODE = TESTING in the file). These features should be used with caution.

listitem.para: Python GMD writer for UTDF one-way downlink range-rate tracking (GMT-8228, GMT-8229). The python utility located in "\utilities\python\navigation\utdf_to_gmd.py" has been updated to add support for one-way downlink range-rate tracking. This utility reads a raw UTDF tracking data and converts it to the GMD format that GMAT can ingest.

section.title: Navigation and Orbit Determination (OD)
section.para: The following navigation and orbit determination capabilities are new to R2026a:

listitem.para: New operational data types for orbit determination (navigation)

GN/DTE one-way downlink range-rate tracking (GMT-8226, GMT-8227). GMAT now supports one-way downlink range-rate measurements in km/sec for orbit determination. This extends the existing RangeRate data type to one-way downlink RangeRate in addition to the existing two-way, and three-way RangeRate measurements. See the Ex_Estimate_RangeRangeRate script in the application samples/Navigation directory.

Batch estimator report hostname value unrecognizable (GMT-8275). Under certain circumstances the GMAT hostname was not properly displayed in the estimator report file. Text associated with the computer hostname is now properly printed out in the estimator report file.

Improve navigation ionosphere modeling performance (GMT-8459, GMT-8168). Refactor of the previous FORTRAN to C (f2c) ionosphere code to improve the speed of the ionosphere correction computation when in use.

section.para: The following navigation and orbit determination capabilities have changed in R2026a:

listitem.para: Corrected a bug in the inner-loop data editing scheme (GMT-7281). A bug affecting the batch estimator inner-loop editing scheme has been corrected. The BatchEstimator inner-loop now functions properly. Users may find the inner-loop editor helps shorten estimation run time by reducing the number of iterations required to converge.

section.para: The following navigation and orbit determination capabilities are deprecated beginning with R2026a:

listitem.para: None.

section.title: Scripting and API Interface
section.para: The following Scripting and API Interface changes were implemented in R2026a:

listitem.para: The following parameters have been prevented from being set in the MissionSequence:

NAIFIdReferenceFrame, SPADSRPInterpolationMethod, SPADDragInterpolationMethod, ModelFile, ModelOffsetX, ModelOffsetY, ModelOffsetZ, ModelRotationX, ModelRotationY, ModelRotationZ, ModelScale, NAIFId, Id

section.title: Beta Capabilities
section.para: The GMAT Optimal Control capability remains a Beta feature for R2026a.

section.title: OpenFramesInterface Visualizations

note.para: The OFI continues to be the primary 3D visualization component in GMAT. OrbitView is deprecated and will no longer be supported. Users are encouraged to migrate their scripts from OrbitView to OpenFramesInterface.

section.para: The complete set of documentation for OFI is available at the OFI Wiki

listitem.para: Corrected issue GMT-8427, OpenFramesInterface New Vector view panel does not resize properly.
Corrected issue GMT-8355, OpenFramesView panel does not display correctly.
Enable OpenFramesInterface font options (GMT-8536).
Corrected issue GMT-6571, SystemTime() fails on rerun when first run was interrupted.

Enabled support for Mac Intel Machines (GMT-8340). On macOS, GMAT is now distributed as a universal application. Future versions of the GMAT Mac application may not fully support the Intel chipset due to third party dependency limitations (e.g., MATLAB interface).
Fixed notarization of the Mac application (GMT-8339).
GUI items don't scale on high DPI displays due to WxWidgets limitation (workaround) (GMT-6125).
Update SWIG to the current release (GMT-8299).
Update the build system to use CMake 4 (GMT-8434).
Resolve missing OSG plugins (GMT-8242/GMT-8523).
Remove or reconfigure PCRE in the dependency configuration (GMT-8530).
Support for Python 3.6 through 3.8 has been discontinued.

section.title: Fixed Issues
section.para: 29 high priority bugs were closed in this release. See the "Critical Issues Fixed in R2026a" report for details. Some significant fixes in R2026a include:

row.entry: GMAT on Linux crashes and report generation fails for external force model script.
Mars exponential atmosphere has no effect.

section.title: Known Issues
section.para: See the "All Known Critical Issues for R2026a" report for a list of all known major issues in R2026a. There are several known issues in this release that we consider to be significant:

row.entry: Windows Defender pop-up may appear preventing users from initially opening the GMAT application and require additional user actions.
